sections.paragraph:
[see note after No. 2]-

(1) to reject (opp. to בָּתַר to choose), Isa 7:15, 16 41:9 Job 34:33. Constr. with an acc. 1Sa 16:1 Psa 118:22 and בְּ Isa 7:15 absol. Job 42:6. It is most often used-(a) of God, as rejecting a people or an individual. Jer 6:30 7:29 14:19 1Sa 15:23.-(b) of men as rejecting God and his precepts, 1Sa 15:23 2Ki 17:15 Amo 2:4.

(2) to despise, to contemn, followed by an acc. Pro 15:32 Job 9:21 followed by בְּ Job 19:18 followed by an acc. of pers. and the gerund of a verb, Job 30:1 absol. שֵׁבֶט מֹאֶמֶת a despiteful tribe, Eze 21:18. Inf. מָאֹס Lam 3:45, subst. aversion, contempt.

Niphal, pass. of No. 1, Isai. 54:6 of No. 2; Psa 15:4, נִמְאָס contemned, to be contemned, rejected (for impiety).

II.

מאס

i.q. מָסַס to melt, to flow abroad, Chald. מְאַךְ = מָכַךְ, and מְאַס, i.q. מְסַס. Not used in Kal.

Niphal, Psa 58:8, יִמָּאֲסֹו בְמֹו־מַיִס “let them melt away like water,” i.e. perish. Job 7:5, עֹורִי רָנַע וַיִמָאֵס “my skin heals up and (again) runs with water.”

[Note. In Thes. מָאַס is given as one article; the meaning here assigned to מָאַס No. II. being there taken as primary.]
